title: rhagodia triandra
intro: Rhagodia triandra, also known as Sea Blanch or Sea purslane, is a succulent shrub native to Australia and New Zealand, commonly found in coastal areas and saltbush flats. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rhagodia triandra include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and potentially aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Rhagodia triandra can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy in modern medicine.
